Fellow 4G leadership candidate; both considered as potential successors to PM Lee Hsien Loong

political_stances.stance_content: Singapore's strongest defence and deterrence is not any weapon system, but its people's collective will to fight for what they believe in.
Small states must choose principles, not sides. Singapore values China's consistent partnership and constructive contributions to regional security.
Having robust conventional capabilities remains core to strong and credible deterrence. Singapore must continue to modernise and upgrade capabilities.
Maintained policy that single mothers receive only 8 of 16 weeks paid maternity leave that married mothers are entitled to.
Singapore is heavily reliant on foreign trade and must maintain open supply chains.

major_achievements.achievement: Awarded President's Scholarship and Singapore Armed Forces Overseas Scholarship

major_achievements.organization: US Army Command and General Staff College
major_achievements.achievement: First foreign student to be conferred the Distinguished Master Strategist Award
Appointed Chief of Army, attaining rank of Major-General
Delivered closing speech at the 22nd Shangri-la Dialogue as Defence Minister

biography_summary: Chan Chun Sing is a Singaporean politician and former major-general who is serving as Coordinating Minister for Public Services and Minister for Defence since 2025. He grew up in a single-parent household in MacPherson. A President's Scholar and SAF Overseas Scholar, he served as Chief of Army (2010-2011) before entering politics in 2011. He has held multiple cabinet portfolios including Education (2021-2025), Trade and Industry (2018-2021), and Social and Family Development (2012-2015). He was also Secretary-General of NTUC (2015-2018) and was considered a leading 4G leadership candidate. A member of the PAP, he has been MP for the Buona Vista division of Tanjong Pagar GRC since 2011.
